verses.text: Nearing Jerusalem, He sends for a donkey and colt, bidding the disciples say, 'The Lord hath need of them.' Royal claim. The King requisitions what He needs for His entry.
Nearing Jerusalem, He sends for a donkey and colt, bidding the disciples say, 'The Lord hath need of them.' Royal claim. The King requisitions what He needs for His entry.
Nearing Jerusalem, He sends for a donkey and colt, bidding the disciples say, 'The Lord hath need of them.' Royal claim. The King requisitions what He needs for His entry.
This fulfils Zechariah: 'thy King cometh... meek, and sitting upon an ass.' Lowly majesty. He enters not on a war-horse but in humble peace.
This fulfils Zechariah: 'thy King cometh... meek, and sitting upon an ass.' Lowly majesty. He enters not on a war-horse but in humble peace.
They spread garments and branches, crying, 'Hosanna to the Son of David; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. For a moment the crowds hail their King.
They spread garments and branches, crying, 'Hosanna to the Son of David; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. For a moment the crowds hail their King.
They spread garments and branches, crying, 'Hosanna to the Son of David; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. For a moment the crowds hail their King.
They spread garments and branches, crying, 'Hosanna to the Son of David; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. For a moment the crowds hail their King.
The city is stirred, 'Who is this?' and the multitude answer, 'Jesus the prophet of Nazareth.' A great question. The coming of Christ moves a whole city to ask who He is.
The city is stirred, 'Who is this?' and the multitude answer, 'Jesus the prophet of Nazareth.' A great question. The coming of Christ moves a whole city to ask who He is.
He casts out the traders, 'My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.' Zeal for worship. Christ will not have His Father's house made a market.
He casts out the traders, 'My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.' Zeal for worship. Christ will not have His Father's house made a market.
He casts out the traders, 'My house shall be called the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.' Zeal for worship. Christ will not have His Father's house made a market.

The children cry Hosanna; to the displeased priests He answers, 'Out of the mouth of babes... thou hast perfected praise.' Childlike worship. God accepts the praise of the simple over the scorn of the proud.

The children cry Hosanna; to the displeased priests He answers, 'Out of the mouth of babes... thou hast perfected praise.' Childlike worship. God accepts the praise of the simple over the scorn of the proud.

The children cry Hosanna; to the displeased priests He answers, 'Out of the mouth of babes... thou hast perfected praise.' Childlike worship. God accepts the praise of the simple over the scorn of the proud.
He curses a leafy fig tree bearing no fruit, and it withers. A living parable. Empty profession, all leaves and no fruit, comes under God's curse.
He curses a leafy fig tree bearing no fruit, and it withers. A living parable. Empty profession, all leaves and no fruit, comes under God's curse.
He curses a leafy fig tree bearing no fruit, and it withers. A living parable. Empty profession, all leaves and no fruit, comes under God's curse.
If ye have faith and doubt not, ye shall move mountains; 'whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.' Faith's power. Believing prayer lays hold of God's strength.
If ye have faith and doubt not, ye shall move mountains; 'whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.' Faith's power. Believing prayer lays hold of God's strength.
Asked by what authority, He asks of John's baptism; afraid to answer, they say they cannot tell, so neither will He. Cornered hypocrites. Those who will not own the truth forfeit the answer.
Asked by what authority, He asks of John's baptism; afraid to answer, they say they cannot tell, so neither will He. Cornered hypocrites. Those who will not own the truth forfeit the answer.

Of two sons, one refused but went, the other promised but went not; the publicans enter the kingdom before the priests. Deeds, not words. Late obedience outweighs empty promise.
Of two sons, one refused but went, the other promised but went not; the publicans enter the kingdom before the priests. Deeds, not words. Late obedience outweighs empty promise.

Of two sons, one refused but went, the other promised but went not; the publicans enter the kingdom before the priests. Deeds, not words. Late obedience outweighs empty promise.
A householder lets his vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by those He set over His people.
A householder lets his vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by those He set over His people.
A householder lets his vineyard, but the husbandmen beat his servants. Abused trust. God's messengers were ill-treated by those He set over His people.
He sends more servants, then his son, whom they slay to seize the inheritance. Crowning crime. They kill the heir himself — a picture of Christ rejected.
He sends more servants, then his son, whom they slay to seize the inheritance. Crowning crime. They kill the heir himself — a picture of Christ rejected.
He sends more servants, then his son, whom they slay to seize the inheritance. Crowning crime. They kill the heir himself — a picture of Christ rejected.
He sends more servants, then his son, whom they slay to seize the inheritance. Crowning crime. They kill the heir himself — a picture of Christ rejected.
The lord will destroy those husbandmen and let the vineyard to others who render the fruit. Just judgment. The kingdom is taken from the faithless and given to the fruitful.
The lord will destroy those husbandmen and let the vineyard to others who render the fruit. Just judgment. The kingdom is taken from the faithless and given to the fruitful.
'The stone which the builders rejected... is become the head of the corner'; it shall grind to powder all who fall on it. Rejected yet exalted. Christ, refused by men, is made the cornerstone by God.
'The stone which the builders rejected... is become the head of the corner'; it shall grind to powder all who fall on it. Rejected yet exalted. Christ, refused by men, is made the cornerstone by God.
'The stone which the builders rejected... is become the head of the corner'; it shall grind to powder all who fall on it. Rejected yet exalted. Christ, refused by men, is made the cornerstone by God.
The priests perceive He speaks of them and seek to seize Him, but fear the multitude. Conviction without repentance. They feel the truth yet harden against it.
The priests perceive He speaks of them and seek to seize Him, but fear the multitude. Conviction without repentance. They feel the truth yet harden against it.
